question.label: Carrying ambiguity
question.name: Where is that ambiguity landing hardest for you right now?
question.description: Follows her specific point about carrying ambiguity to surface what sits underneath it in practice.

answer: I am not fully clear yet on how success is being measured in this new setup.

assessment.note: She names a specific clarity gap around success measures in the new setup; that uncertainty is also mildly draining and constrains growth.

new_queue.label: Success measures
new_queue.name: What feels most undefined about how success is being measured now?

new_queue.label: Support gap
new_queue.name: Where would a bit more support make the biggest difference right now?

new_queue.label: Next step
new_queue.name: Given what we've covered, where do you want to focus first?

history.answer_excerpt: I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
I have been quieter in leadership meetings because I am still figuring out where my voice has most leverage.
I do not think anything is broken, but I do feel less grounded than I did before.
I have been protecting my team from noise, which means I am carrying more ambiguity myself.
I am not fully clear yet on how success is being measured in this new setup.

I am not fully clear yet on how success is being measured in this new setup.
